entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: An important oilseed and cash crop (Arachis hypogaea) used for edible oil and protein

entities.name: STEM ROT
entities.description: A disease caused by Athelia rolfsii that leads to significant yield losses in peanuts

entities.name: ATHELIA ROLFSII
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing stem rot disease in peanuts

entities.name: STEM ROT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of peanut accessions to withstand stem rot infection

entities.name: SUBSP. FASITIGIATE
entities.description: A subspecies of peanut evaluated for its resistance to stem rot

entities.name: SUBSP. HYPOGAEA
entities.description: A subspecies of peanut evaluated for its resistance to stem rot

entities.name: SOUTHERN CHINA
entities.description: An ecological region where peanut accessions showed the lowest disease index for stem rot

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME RESEQUENCING
entities.description: A method used to analyze the genotypes of peanut accessions

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphisms identified as being associated with stem rot resistance

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDY
entities.description: A method used to identify significant SNPs associated with stem rot resistance

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes located near significant SNPs involved in recognition, signal transduction, and defense response

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: A method to improve stem rot resistance in peanuts using genetic markers and candidate genes

entities.name: YIELD LOSSES
entities.description: The reduction in peanut production resulting from stem rot disease

relationships.description: location of accessions with high resistance
